title: A Practical Content Workflow for a Vercel Help Site
content:
## Keep the content model simple
For a small help center, start with repo-based content. A post file can hold the title, slug, excerpt, date, and body.

## Choose one rendering path
Use a single page component for blog posts and feed it either local file content or CMS data. That keeps your design and SEO output consistent.

## Prefer HTTPS fetches for external content
If your editors use a CMS, your Next.js app should fetch the published data over HTTPS in a server component or route handler. You do not need the Node `http` module for this.

## Deploy on every approved change
Pair GitHub with Vercel so content changes get a preview deployment before you merge them to production.
excerpt: A clean starter workflow for managing help-center blog content in a Vercel-hosted Next.js app.
